title: 짝남,짝녀를 꼬시려면 이렇게 하세요 (밀당 아님)
text: Among the topics in my videos that get good views and are frequently asked about, one is wondering about this person's sincerity— people ask a lot about how much they have feelings for me. Honestly, I don't really like that topic. It's because people think, "Even if they don't like me, I can just win them over." But it's not just a matter of knowing this; there is a real method. You just have to make them like you. People usually say that it is difficult to move someone's heart, but I don't agree with that. It's only because you don't do anything that would move the other person's heart. In fact, people's hearts tend to move more easily than you think. Just looking at expressions like "a fickle heart," "sudden excitement," "fickle mind," and "love comes back," you can see that our hearts are constantly changing. So, how exactly do you do that? I will share three methods and one bonus tip. First, regarding attitude, you must not show any "S" (a negative side) from that point on. Trying to force a good impression—if you act too blatantly and overtly just because you want them to like you—makes it too obvious. Since they don't have feelings for you in the first place, there's no way that will look good; it comes across negatively. Then, their dislike will only solidify even more. For example, asking "How do you like me?" is forbidden. Asking "How do you like me?" itself is meaningless. Even if they can't answer "No, not really," they are already thinking, "Why are you acting like this all of a sudden?" and closing their heart even further. Even if they do n't explicitly say it, a typical behavior is mindlessly sending texts like "What are you doing?" or "Knock knock" just because you like this person so much. That is truly the absolute taboo. Also, asking "Are we dating?" and getting upset on your own—asking "Why are you talking to another guy?" or "How can you talk to someone else when I have no feelings for you"—is just a manifestation of impatience. It does absolutely nothing to change this person's mind or make them like you. Another mistake many people make in their desperate efforts is bragging excessively.

text: else when I have no feelings for you"—is just a manifestation of impatience. It does absolutely nothing to change this person's mind or make them like you. Another mistake many people make in their desperate efforts is bragging excessively. I understand that you need to appeal to them first and want to convey that you are a decent person. But the problem is that the way you express that is bragging. For instance, it's something men often do—thinking they can show off their capabilities or their thick wallet—and suddenly saying, "Wow, I invested in stocks this time and it went really well," out of context. Then, the other person just thinks, " Oh, I see." Are you going to give me a tip? Are you going to give me a tip? If you earned 1 million won, are you going to give me 100,000 won? Then you can find sincerity and applause. Maybe they might start to like you a little. It is n't good for men to show off their capabilities too much. And the things women often brag about are things like saying they went to a fancy dressy place or that they got their number taken while going out today, or that their ex- boyfriend contacted them and was badmouthing them, or that they dumped someone else but other decent people keep approaching them. Okay, this works on someone who has feelings for you. But to someone who doesn't, it makes them think, "So what? Are you a paid shill?" It works if they have even a little bit of feelings, but if they don't, honestly, it's a bit annoying. "So what?" Another common mistake is dealing with in- laws. They think, "There is no tree that won't fall if you strike it ten times," so they try striking ten times. But during those ten strikes, all affection is completely drained. It's not a secret code; it's a person. Also, both men and women fail to distinguish between seduction and looking cheap. Thinking that physical contact will make them develop feelings for them, they drink alcohol or touch for no reason... People keep trying to touch someone they aren't interested in. While this happens more often with men, there are women who become more affectionate and touchy after drinking. However, if they aren't interested in you, it just makes them feel like you look really cheap. So, the priority is to establish these taboos first. title: 짝남,짝녀를 꼬시려면 이렇게 하세요 (밀당 아님)
text: o, the priority is to establish these taboos first. Second, you need to act the exact opposite of the image they have of you. What is truly important in a relationship is the image you project to the other person. It is true that people are attracted to a specific image rather than liking someone for their true nature. If you look at this, the answer to why they don't like you becomes clear: you are not the image they want. They likely have a specific image they prefer. But what if they think, "I'm not like that," or "They don't approach me because they see me as too aloof"? Then what do you do? Act innocent. There are many cases where they think of you as too comfortable. In that case, you need to intentionally create awkward situations from time to time. Be quiet, suddenly do nothing, and if they have become too comfortable because you talked too much before, show them that it's a bit awkward when you meet. Instead of thinking, "Will they not like me even if I'm hot?", they might look at you in a different light, and at the very least, it creates room for them to think about you in a new light. It means that the thought you didn't consider as a romantic interest starts to crack little by little, eventually becoming the catalyst for a breakup. When that happens, contact is usually cut off or they drift apart. This is because that image was your utility value. For example, let's say you're someone who buys meals, but you only bought meals and acted like a pushover. If I stop buying you meals, your utility value has dropped, so contact is cut off. Suppose a man likes a woman, but he comes over to fix her computer and spends hours diligently working on it. In that case, he's just a pushover. Then his image will solidify as just a computer fixer. Unless he does that over and over again, that image won't solidify. Instead, say, "Hey, I fixed this, but this isn't working," and go to a repair shop together—maybe in Yongsan. Then have a meal together and talk about other things. That's better. That way, you have a justification, you don't have that image of just a computer fixer, and you create an image of someone you can hang out with. What about a woman who had a cute image but is actually good at sports? It depends on the sport. Imagine someone who had a cute image but suddenly does martial arts, suddenly wraps up in Jiu-Jitsu, and suddenly applies a technique. He's crazy, his charm is off the charts. It's like how guys who dress casually look different when they show up in a suit after attending a wedding, right? If he was just a friend you had no particular feelings for, but he suddenly pops up like that, his image changes, so you're more attracted to him. This is just the beginning. Once these two things are established, there are things you need to do little by little. That is, giving subtle hints. When he doesn't really have feelings for you, make them show it clearly. I mentioned earlier that negative feelings only grow stronger because you're making an overly aggressive move. He keeps getting subtly bothered, and you start to feel something akin to romantic feelings. Then, you gradually match that and make it more blatant, and later, if he asks, "Do you want to watch a movie?", it all explodes. I consider this to be stage 5. Just because someone doesn't like you doesn't mean it ends there. If someone says they don't like you, that's stage 1. It's not the end; you continue to playfully tap into subtle emotions, and that subtlety builds up little by little. I call this stage 3, the accumulation of emotions. When these emotions continue to accumulate, a slightly more private and personal feeling emerges... You have to express yourself openly and gradually when she walks in. The reason guys fail when confessing is that they do n't consider this stage. Regardless of whether there is interest or not—in reality, most of the time there isn't—they throw out a direct confession right away, starting from stage 4. You need to build up subtle emotions, and only after those feelings have gradually accumulated do you throw out the confession. Then, what happens is that you reach stage 5: you gain certainty about those subtle feelings that have been building up. You think, "Ah, so this is because she likes me," or "I like this." To subtly signal my interest to a girl, I would walk past her and say, "I just tap her on the shoulder and go." But instead of tapping her, I would lightly brush off the dust. I'd say there's dust there—even if there really isn't—but I'd brush it off anyway and pretend to be cool as I walked past. Literally, you throw a jab. Think of it as throwing another punch. And around that time, if you ask, "Do you want to grab a meal or go somewhere together?" they'll say, " Okay," and follow along. It's a process of building rapport starting from scratch. When you establish common ground, you can do a high-five. I mean, guys do that a lot, right? I'm telling you, it's good if you do it naturally to a girl you're interested in. But the problem is, if guys think a high-five works, they'll just keep doing nothing but high-fives. If you do that, women will be like, "Wow, seriously, they get sick of you." You can't block a jab unless it lands from various angles. Winning over someone who doesn't like you is actually a long-term battle, and patience is crucial. You have to endure the time they don't like you very well. You endure it, and then, little by little, nibble nibble nibble nibble, accumulating emotions. You express yourself when you reach a certain level. Don't be pushy from the start; make your move at the right moment. But many people think, "If they don't like me, so be it. If not, never mind." They just push right from the beginning, thinking, "If it works, great; if not, oh well." Then nothing works out. They end up moving on like Jehovah's Sin for The, saying, "I didn't really like them that much either." We should n't become such pathetic people. text: decent guy" come out of their mouths, they can't help but pay attention. There isn't one. People say that the way to become a verified person is through this. I could say, " Actually, I like this person, can you help me out?" Or, simply maintaining a good reputation is also a very safe and decent method. The three points I mentioned earlier are the key, and having the support ministry of those around you as a bonus is the icing on the cake. [Music]
